extraction_reasoning: The litigation table separates matters filed by and against each entity: I counted only the “Against” rows, with criminal proceedings of 0 (Company, Directors, Promoters, Subsidiaries and KMP/SMP), statutory/regulatory proceedings of 0, and tax proceedings against the Company and Subsidiaries of 7 (the Company row has 7 and the Subsidiaries row is Nil); all “By” rows and personal promoter/director matters were excluded from the aggregate amount. The quantified amount against the Company and Subsidiaries is ₹9,173 million for the Company plus Nil for Subsidiaries, or ₹917.30 crore after conversion; promoter and director amounts were excluded. The contingent-liabilities table is dated March 31, 2025 and has no stated Total row, so I summed the disclosed ₹0.57 million income-tax amount and ₹91.69 million GST amount to ₹92.26 million, or ₹9.226 crore. Concentration is company-wide top-10 customer revenue of 46.59% for Fiscal 2025, using revenue from operations as the denominator; no company-wide top-five percentage is disclosed. The source currency unit is ₹ million, converted to crore by dividing by 10.
